question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getClients()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nodeSupplier.refresh().collect(Collectors.toList()) at line 133 where the entire node list is collected before building clients, so that if the attacker can delay or manipulate the refresh() stream (e.g., via slow REST API responses), the client pool is built with a stale or incomplete node list, causing transactions to be routed to nodes that may have been compromised or removed from the active Hashgraph network?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: publish(PublishRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of transaction content validation in publish() before calling getTransactionResponse() at line 67, allowing a crafted Transaction object with a manipulated memo field, transfer amounts, or token IDs to be submitted directly to the Hashgraph network and permanently recorded in its immutable history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: publish(PublishRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the reactive chain's lack of idempotency guarantees in publish(), where a network error after transaction submission but before response receipt causes the caller to retry the same transaction, resulting in duplicate transactions being submitted and recorded in Hashgraph history, effectively injecting phantom entries?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: execute(Client, Transaction<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Mono.fromFuture() at line 118 where the CompletableFuture returned by executeAsync() is not cancelled on subscription disposal, causing a transaction to continue executing even after the reactive chain is cancelled (e.g., due to timeout), resulting in an untracked transaction being recorded in Hashgraph history without the monitor's knowledge?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: toClient(List<NodeProperties>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the NodeAddressBook.newBuilder().addAllNodeAddress(nodeAddresses).build().toByteString() at lines 154-157 where the address book is serialized and then deserialized via fromBytes() at line 160, introducing a serialization round-trip that could be exploited if the protobuf deserialization has vulnerabilities, allowing a crafted node address to survive the round-trip and redirect transactions to a malicious endpoint in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: toClient(List<NodeProperties>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nodes.stream().filter(n -> accountIds.contains(n.getAccountId())) at line 151, where the filter uses a string-based accountId comparison, to inject a NodeProperties with an accountId that matches an existing node but has a different endpoint, causing the address book to contain a node address pointing to an attacker-controlled endpoint while appearing to be a legitimate node in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: publish(PublishRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the clients.elementAt(clientIndex) at line 66 where the Flux is cached and elementAt() does not re-subscribe, causing the same client instance to be shared across concurrent publish() calls without synchronization, leading to race conditions in the SDK client's internal state that result in transactions being submitted with incorrect node targeting and corrupted Hashgraph history entries?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: processTransactionResponse(Client, PublishRequest, TransactionResponse)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the builder.transactionRecord(r).receipt(r.receipt) at line 107 where the receipt is extracted from the TransactionRecord returned by a potentially compromised node, allowing the node to return a record with a manipulated receipt status (e.g., SUCCESS for a failed transaction), causing the monitor to falsely record a successful Hashgraph history entry for a transaction that was never actually executed?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTransactionResponse(PublishRequest, Client)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the transaction.setNodeAccountIds(node.getAccountIds()) at line 86 where setNodeAccountIds() mutates the shared Transaction object, causing a race condition when the same Transaction is used in concurrent publish() calls, resulting in one call's node assignment overwriting another's and submitting the transaction to an unintended node that manipulates Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: publish(PublishRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of transaction signature verification in publish() before submission, where a Transaction object with an invalid or forged signature is submitted to the Hashgraph network, and the monitor records the submission as successful based solely on the network response, creating a false entry in the monitor's view of Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: toClient(List<NodeProperties>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Client.forNetwork(Map.of()) at line 159 where the client is initialized with an empty network map before being configured, creating a window where the client has no valid network configuration, and if a concurrent publish() call uses this partially-initialized client, it may submit a transaction to an undefined endpoint that is not part of the legitimate Hashgraph network?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: publish(PublishRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the PublishScenario scenario = request.getScenario() at line 63 where the scenario object is mutable and shared, allowing concurrent modification of scenario properties (e.g., timeout, logResponse) during transaction execution, causing transactions to be submitted with altered parameters that affect how they are recorded in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: execute(Client, Transaction<?>)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the synchronous Mono.fromCallable(() -> executable.execute(client)) at line 120 where the blocking call is executed on a reactive scheduler thread, causing thread pool exhaustion under high load, which delays transaction submission timing and allows an attacker to manipulate the relative ordering of transactions in Hashgraph history by controlling the timing of competing submissions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: processTransactionResponse(Client, PublishRequest, TransactionResponse)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the TransactionRecordQuery().setQueryPayment(Hbar.from(1, HbarUnit.HBAR)) at line 105 where the fixed 1 HBAR payment is hardcoded, allowing an attacker to manipulate network fee conditions so that the query payment is insufficient, causing the record query to fail and the monitor to fall back to an incomplete response, creating gaps in the monitor's view of Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: close()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the close() method at line 43 where clients.subscribe(client -> client.close()) is called without waiting for in-flight transactions to complete, causing transactions that have been submitted to the Hashgraph network but not yet confirmed to be abandoned, creating untracked entries in Hashgraph history that the monitor never records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: publish(PublishRequest)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Mono.empty() return at line 58 when publishProperties.isEnabled() is false, where the publish() method silently drops the request without any error or audit trail, allowing an attacker who can toggle the enabled flag to suppress legitimate transactions from being submitted to the Hashgraph network, creating gaps in the immutable history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getClients()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the flatMapMany(nodes -> Flux.range(0, publishProperties.getClients()).flatMap(i -> Mono.defer(() -> Mono.just(toClient(nodes))))) at lines 133-135 where all clients share the same nodes list reference, so that if the nodes list is modified after client creation (e.g., via nodeSupplier refresh), the clients' network configurations become inconsistent, causing transactions to be routed to nodes that are no longer part of the validated Hashgraph network?
